title: Dryopteris schizoloma
taxonomy: Dryopteris schizoloma is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes about 200 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is a part of the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of vascular plants that includes many species of ferns and their relatives. Dryopteris schizoloma is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The classification of Dryopteris schizoloma is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Dryopteridaceae, genus Dryopteris, and species D. schizoloma.
uses: Dryopteris schizoloma is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in China. intro: Dryopteris schizoloma, also known as Schizoloma schizoloma, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris schizoloma include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
